_comment: Optional airband receiver config for maia-httpd. Copy to /root/airband.json on the Pluto. If absent, maia-httpd uses these same built-in defaults. samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 128 / 7 = 15625 sps at 14 MHz). channels_hz: up to 21 absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2. gain: fixed manual 73 dB (AD9361 manual ceiling for this band; range is [-1 1 73]). This is only safe because an external selective filter attenuates the strong local 118.050 MHz transmitter that previously drove the wideband ADC into hard clipping (~13% of samples -> broadband intermod, raised floor + spurs). With that filter the band is quiet (RSSI ~85 dB, 0 clipping at 73 dB) so max gain maximizes sensitivity for weak/distant channels. WITHOUT that external filter, do NOT use 73 here -- drop to ~48 dB (the highest gain that did not overload the bare front end). The residual in-band spur comb (120 MHz = 40 MHz ref 3rd harmonic) is hardware, not gain-related.
